codeReview.summary: The project-scoped Context Pack path remains correct, but the new stale-state reset only watches `session.sessionId`. Because the real reload identity is `provider + sessionId`, a provider swap that reuses the same sessionId can still leak the prior session’s conversation/summary/anchor state until the new fetch completes.

issues.description: .onChange only tracks `session.sessionId`, but the reload task is keyed by `ConversationRecord.stableId(provider: session.provider, sessionId: session.sessionId)`. If the detail view is reused for a different provider with the same sessionId, the old conversation/summary/context-pack anchor state is not cleared before the async reload, so stale UI can still leak across rapid switches.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `context-pack-ui-worker` procedure says Session Detail assertions must instantiate `SessionDetailView` and assert visibility/order/presentation through the rendered view path, but `AgentLensTests/Active/ContextPackSessionDetailSurfaceTests.swift` still contains only database/state assertions and comments. `SessionDetailView(` and `inspect()` do not appear in that file, so the contract can still appear satisfied without exercising the real view surface.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Grep over `AgentLensTests/Active/ContextPackSessionDetailSurfaceTests.swift` found no `SessionDetailView(` or `inspect()` matches; the `test_initialRenderDoesNotLeakPriorSessionState` body only checks fetched conversations and comments on the expected behavior.

summary: Fail: the project-scoped assembly fix holds, but the stale-state reset is still keyed too narrowly and can miss provider swaps that reuse the same sessionId.
